title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 5: Logic & Patterns
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Maria has 14 stickers and her brother gives her 9 more. How many stickers does Maria have now?
questions.solution: Add 14 and 9 to get 23 stickers.

questions.question_text: Look at the pattern of shapes. What comes next after circle, square, triangle, circle, square, triangle, circle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='20' cy='30' r='12'/><rect x='50' y='18' width='24' height='24'/><polygon points='105,42 117,18 129,42'/><circle cx='160' cy='30' r='12'/><rect x='190' y='18' width='24' height='24'/><polygon points='245,42 257,18 269,42'/><text x='285' y='36' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Repeating pattern of circle, square, triangle with a question mark at the end.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every three shapes. After circle comes square.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 3:15 and lasts 45 minutes. What time does it end?
questions.solution: 3:15 plus 45 minutes is 4:00.

questions.question_text: How many sides does the shape below have in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,15 100,45 85,95 35,95 20,45'/></svg>
questions.solution: The pentagon has 5 sides.

questions.question_text: Leo buys a pencil for 3 dollars and a notebook for 5 dollars. He pays with a 10-dollar bill. How much change does he get?
questions.solution: Pencil plus notebook is 8 dollars. 10 minus 8 is 2 dollars change.

questions.question_text: In a row of 5 children, Sam is third from the left. How many children are to the right of Sam?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='25' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='75' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='125' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='175' cy='30' r='15'/><circle cx='225' cy='30' r='15'/><text x='118' y='35' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>S</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Five circles in a row representing children, with the middle one labeled S for Sam.
questions.solution: If Sam is position 3 out of 5, then positions 4 and 5 are to his right, which is 2 children.

questions.question_text: What is the value of 7 times 8 minus 15?
questions.solution: 7 times 8 is 56. 56 minus 15 is 41.

questions.question_text: A ribbon is 90 centimeters long. Anna cuts it into 3 equal pieces. How long is each piece?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='12' width='220' height='16'/><line x1='83' y1='8' x2='83' y2='32'/><line x1='156' y1='8' x2='156' y2='32'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle representing a ribbon divided into three equal parts.
questions.solution: 90 divided by 3 is 30 centimeters per piece.

questions.question_text: In the number sequence 2, 5, 8, 11, 14, what is the next number?
questions.solution: Each term increases by 3. 14 plus 3 is 17.

questions.question_text: How many small squares make up the large rectangle shown in the picture?
questions.image_description: A 6 by 3 grid of small squares forming a rectangle.
questions.solution: 6 columns times 3 rows equals 18 squares.

questions.question_text: Three friends run a race. Ben finishes before Carla. Anna finishes after Carla. Who finishes first?
questions.solution: Order from first to last is Ben, Carla, Anna. Ben is first.

questions.question_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Tom eats 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.image_description: A circle divided into 8 equal slices.
questions.solution: 8 minus 3 is 5 slices left out of 8, which is 5/8.

questions.question_text: A box contains 4 packs of cookies. Each pack has 6 cookies. If 9 cookies are eaten, how many are left?
questions.solution: 4 times 6 is 24 cookies total. 24 minus 9 is 15 cookies left.

questions.question_text: Pia goes to bed at 9:00 pm and wakes up at 6:30 am. How many hours does she sleep?
questions.solution: From 9:00 pm to 6:00 am is 9 hours. Adding 30 more minutes gives 9 hours 30 minutes.

questions.question_text: How many triangles are shown in the figure below?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='70,15 15,105 125,105'/><line x1='70' y1='15' x2='70' y2='105'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle split down the middle by a vertical line, forming smaller triangles.
questions.solution: There are 2 small triangles plus 1 large triangle, making 3 triangles in total.

questions.question_text: Ella has three 5-dollar bills and four 1-dollar bills. A toy costs 14 dollars. How much money does she have left after buying the toy?
questions.solution: Total money is 15 plus 4, which is 19 dollars. 19 minus 14 is 5 dollars.

questions.question_text: Four cards show the numbers 2, 4, 7, and 9. You pick two different cards and add their numbers. Which total below is impossible to make?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='40' height='50'/><rect x='70' y='15' width='40' height='50'/><rect x='130' y='15' width='40' height='50'/><rect x='190' y='15' width='40' height='50'/><text x='25' y='48' font-size='20' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2</text><text x='85' y='48' font-size='20' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>4</text><text x='145' y='48' font-size='20' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>7</text><text x='205' y='48' font-size='20' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four cards showing the numbers 2, 4, 7, and 9.
questions.solution: Possible pair sums are 2+4=6, 2+7=9, 2+9=11, 4+7=11, 4+9=13, 7+9=16. The sum 10 is not possible.

questions.question_text: A row of beads follows the pattern red, red, blue, red, red, blue. If the pattern continues, what color is the 20th bead?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='15' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='40' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='65' cy='20' r='10' fill='#cccccc'/><circle cx='90' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='115' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='10' fill='#cccccc'/><circle cx='165' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='190' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='215' cy='20' r='10' fill='#cccccc'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A repeating pattern of two light beads followed by one shaded bead.
questions.solution: The pattern has length 3. Position 20 divided by 3 gives remainder 2, so it is the second bead in the group, which is red.

questions.question_text: The sum of three consecutive whole numbers is 48. What is the smallest of these numbers?
questions.solution: Three consecutive numbers summing to 48 have a middle number of 16. The smallest is 15.

questions.question_text: A square has a perimeter of 36 cm. What is the length of one side?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='80' height='80'/></svg>
questions.solution: A square has 4 equal sides. 36 divided by 4 is 9 cm.

questions.question_text: In Kim's drawer there are 5 red socks and 4 blue socks, all mixed up. Without looking, what is the smallest number of socks she must take out to be sure of getting a matching pair?
questions.solution: With only 2 colors, taking 3 socks guarantees at least 2 of one color.

questions.question_text: A rectangular garden is 12 meters long and 5 meters wide. What is the distance all the way around the garden?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='140' height='60'/><text x='80' y='15' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>12 m</text><text x='165' y='55' font-size='12' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>5 m</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle labeled 12 meters by 5 meters.
questions.solution: Perimeter is 2 times (12 plus 5), which equals 2 times 17, which equals 34 meters.

questions.question_text: Four children Ana, Ben, Cal, and Dee sit in seats 1, 2, 3, 4 in a row. Ana sits in seat 2. Ben sits immediately to the left of Ana. Cal sits immediately to the right of Ana. Which seat does Dee sit in?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='50' height='35'/><rect x='70' y='15' width='50' height='35'/><rect x='130' y='15' width='50' height='35'/><rect x='190' y='15' width='50' height='35'/><text x='32' y='38' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>1</text><text x='92' y='38' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2</text><text x='152' y='38' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>3</text><text x='212' y='38' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>4</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Four seats in a row labeled 1 through 4.
questions.solution: Ana is in seat 2, so Ben is in seat 1 and Cal is in seat 3. The only seat left for Dee is seat 4.

questions.question_text: Noah thinks of a number. He doubles it, then adds 5, and gets 21. What was Noah's number?
questions.solution: 21 minus 5 is 16. 16 divided by 2 is 8.
